entry.title: Re: how to export ldm from sap power designer to excel or...
entry.content:
Expanding on Ondrej's answer here - why should you use a list report instead of exporting from a list of objects?

There are several reasons for using a List Report instead of a list of Objects (on the Model menu):

  1. a List report can include information from related objects - a list of model Objects cannot do this. A List report of Entity Attributes can include the description of the Entity that owns an Attribute, and properties from the Domain an Attribute references.
  2. A List report can be created for additional types of Objects, such as Mappings
  3. A List Report is an Object in the Model; it can be copied to another Model.
  4. You can save read-only snapshots (known as 'Result Sets') of the output from a List Report, allowing you to see how things have changed over time (especially if you add a meaningful comment to the snapshot).

entry.title: Re: how to export ldm from sap power designer to excel or...
entry.content: and you can automate the generation of a List Report with a model extension, automatically creating a result set when you do so

entry.title: Avoid hard coding time periods in SAC Planning data actions
entry.content:
We have the following data action to make a forecast for the next remaining months based on the index of the previous months. The index is calculated on past months. This index is used for the calculation of the future months. The below script will calculate the specific "PERIOD_FORECAST" (for example period 10 or 11 or 12) by multiplying the Budget for period 10 times the calculated index over the past 9 periods:

 

// Context
MEMBERSET [d/Measures] = "AMOUNT"
MEMBERSET [d/BEDRIJFSNR] = %COMPANY_FILTER%
MEMBERSET [d/Date] = %PERIOD_FORECAST%
MEMBERSET [d/NIV4] = %RUBRIEK%
 
// Empty data
DATA([d/Date] = %PERIOD_FORECAST%) = 0
 
// Formules
DATA([d/Date] = %PERIOD_FORECAST%) = 
((RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202301") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202302") 
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202303") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202304") 
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202305") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202306")
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202307") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202308")
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE%, [d/Date] = "202309"))
/
(RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202301") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202302") 
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202303") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202304") 
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202305") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202306")
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202307") + RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202308")
+ RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = "202309")))
* RESULTLOOKUP([d/Version] = %BASELINE_INDEX_VERSIE_VERGELIJK%, [d/Date] = %PERIOD_FORECAST%)

We would like to avoid the hard coded "2023xx" and replace it with a flexible solution

entry.title: Extract KNVV Customer Master Sales Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program
entry.content:
ABAP Program -  Z_KNVV_CUSTOMER  

  • Program Type - ABAP 
  • ABAP Program Name - Z_KNVV_CUST 
  • Tcode - ZHM_KNVV_C  
  • Entity Description - Customer Master Sales Data 
  • Output File Location - “C:\tmp” 
  • Output File Name- KNVV.CSV 
  • Output File Type - CSV (Column with Semicolons Separator (;)) 
  • SAP Table Name - KNVV, KNB1, KNA1
  • List Of Fields to Be Extracted - 
  • KUNNR 
  • VKBUR 
  • KONDA 
  • WAERS 
  • INCO1 
  • VERSG 
  • KDGRP 

-------ABAP PROGRAM START FROM HERE----------

REPORT Z_KNVV_CUSTOMER.

TYPE-POOLS: VRM.

DATA: NAME TYPE VRM_ID,
LIST TYPE VRM_VALUES,
VALUE LIKE LINE OF LIST.


PARAMETERS: ENTITY(10) AS LISTBOX VISIBLE LENGTH 10.
PARAMETERS : OPATH TYPE STRING DEFAULT '\\SERVERNAME\tmp\KNVV.csv'.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.

NAME = 'ENTITY'.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK01'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK01'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK75'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK75'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK21'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK21'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK31'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK31'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK41'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK41'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK02'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK02'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK73'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK73'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK04'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK04'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK61'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK61'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

CALL FUNCTION 'VRM_SET_VALUES' EXPORTING ID = NAME VALUES = LIST.

START-OF-SELECTION.

type-pools:TRUXS.

data: begin of itab occurs 0,
KUNNR like KNVV-KUNNR,
VKBUR like KNVV-VKBUR,
KONDA like KNVV-KONDA,
WAERS like KNVV-WAERS,
INCO1 like KNVV-INCO1,
VERSG like KNVV-VERSG,
KDGRP like KNVV-KDGRP,
BUKRS like KNB1-BUKRS,
end of itab.

data: itab1 type TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.
SELECT KNVV~KUNNR
KNVV~VKBUR
KNVV~KONDA
KNVV~WAERS
KNVV~INCO1
KNVV~VERSG
KNVV~KDGRP
KNB1~BUKRS
from KNVV INNER JOIN KNB1 ON KNVV~KUNNR = KNB1~KUNNR
INNER JOIN KNA1 ON KNB1~KUNNR = KNA1~KUNNR
into table itab
WHERE KNB1~BUKRS = ENTITY.

CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT'
EXPORTING
I_FIELD_SEPERATOR = ','
TABLES
I_TAB_SAP_DATA = itab
CHANGING
I_TAB_CONVERTED_DATA = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
CONVERSION_FAILED = 1
OTHERS = 2
.
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.

CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
EXPORTING
filename = OPATH
TABLES
data_tab = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
OTHERS = 1.

entry.title: Re: EML- READ Entity using partial key fields
entry.content:
Hey.

Well, EML does not support SELECT statements.

If you want to stick to the EML language, you might need to do the following:
1. Create a Root entity with key1, key2
2. Create a Child entity with key1, key2, key3
3. Use READ ENTITIES to read the root entity with the full key (key1, key2) with an association to the Child entries.

This way you'll be able to fetch all the child result data "using the child entity partial key".

This approach might have some benefits if you are making use of Draft features. Handling draft features with SELECT statements will become a challenge.

entry.title: Where can a database administrators start learning SAP HANA user management?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Implementing SAP HANA User Management learning journey

Overview
In this learning journey, you will learn about users, user groups, role, role groups, privileges in SAP HANA Cloud and SAP HANA Platform. This learning journey equips database administrators with essential skills to create, modify, and delete users, manage roles, and control access.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, you will be able to:

  • Perform SAP HANA user management, including creation, modification, and deletion of users, and assignment of appropriate roles and privileges.
  • Manage roles and privileges to control access to critical data and functions within SAP HANA.

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Authorizations ME51N - I want to disable Save Action
entry.content:
in my role i have transactions ME51N/ME52N/ME53N.

I want to disable on users to make Save - Create PR , but i want to have access to Hold / Park the PR 

How to disable the Save Button Activity ?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Authorizations ME51N - I want to disable Save Action
entry.content:
No, unfortunately this is not possible, PR document is an initial "proposal" which has limited options, this needs to be looked at within the PO (and not PR) context:

> What I think may help here is to understand the purpose of each of the PR transactions:

======

ME51N > Create PR

ME52N > Update / Edit PR

ME53N > Read-only access to PR.

> ME51N and ME52N will both allow editing / saving, ME53N will be read-only.

(PO transactions will be similarly "split", but some fields can be obscured / locked with your own logic in BADI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST or other, and saving will be required).

======

For the stated requirement, you can only have one (ability to change and save) or the other (read only), unfortunately there is no block to saving (while saving is needed to move the document to the next stage).

There is no User Exit / BADI for PRs, as they are only *intermediary* documents - an intention to buy which is then converted to PO (which is the main document).

If the requirement to park documents is required, they must create the PO and use the park option (if available), then look to lock the field(s) they want via User Exit / BADI coding, consult with your in-house ABAPers to realise this if they so choose;

*or*

Don't convert the PR to PO until they are ready to order.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Build Work Zone | Server IP address could not found
entry.content:
Hello Khalid,

Please take a look at this. 

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3392044

Cause

On BTP, the URL segment can't exceed 63 characters.
When using destination URL - Tunneled Access,  the total length of the destination URL pattern<subdomain>-sapdelim-<runtime-destination-name> is more than 63 characters.

Resolution

Shorten the length of the URL.
It is impossible to change the subdomain name once it is created, please shorten the length of runtime destination name, make sure the total length of the destination URL pattern<subdomain>-sapdelim-<runtime-destination-name> is less than 63 characters.

Thank you

Julius

entry.title: How to explore the theming base parameters?
entry.content:
In this blog I will briefly explain the structure of the theming base parameters and give you a tip how you can explore this structure using the UI theme designer.

Most of the parameters are color values, but you will also find dimensions and font parameters in the theming base content. Many, though not all, are available for theming. This means you can modify them within a custom theme in the UI theme designer. However, some parameters are protected and cannot be customized.

You can find the theming base content here: SAP Theming Base Content

There are three types of parameters:

  • Internal Reference Parameters: They build the theme palette and are the DNA of a theme. The parameters are usually not directly referenced in the CSS style classes but used throughout the theme to define other parameters. Internal reference parameters are protected and cannot be modified in the UI theme designer.
  • Main Parameters:  These are high-level parameters which characterize a theme and are connected to several component parameters. Most of them are available for theming. They have the biggest impact when you create a theme and allow quick themeability of SAP applications.
  • Component Parameters: These parameters define the design of individual UI components for example the shell, an application page, buttons, inputs any many more. Component parameters generally have less impact on other parameters than the main parameters.

How to find parameters in the UI theme designer?

In the UI theme designer you find different levels of theming:

  • Quick theming offers the main parameters.
  • Detailed theming provides the parameters for the most important components in a form-based layout.
  • Expert theming lists all parameters available for theming and includes a set of tools to assist you in finding specific parameters.

How to find parameters for a component in expert theming?

  1. Select expert theming.
  2. Open the tag view.
  3. Select the component, e.g. Button.

How to find dependent parameters?

  1. Open expert theming.
  2. Search for the parameter.

How to see if a parameter is calculated from other parameters?

  1. Select expert theming.
  2. Search for the parameter.
  3. Examine the value. In expert theming you find the Less function for a parameter.

Wrap up

In this blog you got an overview of the theming base content structure and learned how to explore the structure in the UI theme designer.

Further information

  • Theming Base Content GitHub Repository
  • Parameter Explorer
  • SAP Fiori for Web: Theming
  • SAP Fiori for Web: Design Tokens

entry.title: Range Compress for dump SAPSQL_STMNT_TOO_LARGE
entry.content:
Introduction

When the size of the Range (as Selection Condition) is too big, system gives short-dump SAPSQL_STMNT_TOO_LARGE.

This function module will decrease the range lines without loosing data.

 

Details

SQL statements has a size limit for Condition Data. When ranges with line more than ~30.000 are used, system can not transfer the condition data to the Database and gives short-dump SAPSQL_STMNT_TOO_LARGE. 

 

For this issue "for all entries" statement is proposed but this statement causes performance problems.

 

The best way is to re-orginize the range data. If multiple line values are in an order, then it can be descreased to one line with operator "Between".

 

Range Compres function module will do this summerization automatically.

 

Example:

Range data Before Compress:

Sign        Option   Low        High

I              EQ          100021 

I              EQ          100024 

I              EQ          100023 

I              EQ          100022 

I              EQ          100028 

I              EQ          100034 

I              EQ          100030 

I              EQ          100031 

I              EQ          100035 

I              EQ          100026 

I              EQ          100037 

I              EQ          100036 

 

 

 Range data After Compress:

Sign        Option   Low        High

I              BT           100021  100024

I              EQ          100026 

I              EQ          100028 

I              BT           100030  100031

I              BT           100034  100037

 

Function Module Coding

This function module is dynamic and can be used for any type of range.

FUNCTION zveta_range_compress.
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  TABLES
*"      T_ITAB
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*Use the minimize Range Data for performance issues and eliminate 
*short dump SAPSQL_STMNT_TOO_LARGE

  DATA : lrs_itab TYPE REF TO data, " Local Ref. Structure
         lrt_itab TYPE REF TO data, " Local Ref. Table
         lrv_low  TYPE REF TO data, " Local Ref. Variable
         lrv_high TYPE REF TO data, " Local Ref. Variable
         lrv_wa_l TYPE REF TO data, " Local Ref. Variable
         lrv_wa_h TYPE REF TO data, " Local Ref. Variable
         lrv_tmp  TYPE REF TO data. " Local Ref. Variable

  CONSTANTS : c_low(3) VALUE 'LOW'.

  FIELD-SYMBOLS : <fs_it>       TYPE STANDARD TABLE,
                  <fs>          TYPE any,
                  <fs_wa>       TYPE any,
                  <fs_low>      TYPE any,
                  <fs_high>     TYPE any,
                  <fs_v_low>    TYPE any,
                  <fs_v_high>   TYPE any,
                  <fs_v_higher> TYPE any,
                  <fs_wa_low>   TYPE any,
                  <fs_wa_high>  TYPE any.


  CHECK t_itab[] IS NOT INITIAL.

  ASSIGN t_itab TO <fs>.
  CREATE DATA lrs_itab LIKE <fs>.
  CREATE DATA lrt_itab LIKE TABLE OF <fs>.

  ASSIGN lrs_itab->* TO <fs_wa>.
  ASSIGN lrt_itab->* TO <fs_it>.

**********************************************************************
  SORT t_itab BY (c_low).
  DELETE ADJACENT DUPLICATES FROM t_itab COMPARING (c_low).
  READ TABLE t_itab ASSIGNING <fs_wa> INDEX 1.
  ASSIGN COMPONENT 'LOW' OF STRUCTURE <fs_wa> TO <fs_low>.
**********************************************************************

**********************************************************************
  CREATE DATA lrv_low  LIKE <fs_low>.
  CREATE DATA lrv_high LIKE <fs_low>.
  CREATE DATA lrv_tmp  LIKE <fs_low>.
  CREATE DATA lrv_wa_l  LIKE <fs_low>.
  CREATE DATA lrv_wa_h  LIKE <fs_low>.

  ASSIGN lrv_low->*  TO <fs_v_low>.
  ASSIGN lrv_high->* TO <fs_v_high>.
  ASSIGN lrv_tmp->*  TO <fs_v_higher>.
  ASSIGN lrv_wa_l->* TO <fs_wa_low>.
  ASSIGN lrv_wa_h->* TO <fs_wa_high>.
**********************************************************************

  <fs_v_low> = <fs_low> .

  PERFORM increase_1 USING <fs_low> CHANGING <fs_v_higher>.

  LOOP AT t_itab ASSIGNING <fs> FROM 2.
    ASSIGN COMPONENT 'LOW' OF STRUCTURE <fs> TO <fs_low>.

    IF <fs_low> EQ <fs_v_higher>.
      "Aralık değeri bulundu
      <fs_v_high> = <fs_low>.

      PERFORM increase_1 USING <fs_low> CHANGING <fs_v_higher>.

    ELSE.
      IF <fs_v_high> IS ASSIGNED AND
         <fs_v_high> IS NOT INITIAL.
        <fs_wa> = 'IBT'.
      ELSE .
        <fs_wa> = 'IEQ'.
      ENDIF.

      ASSIGN COMPONENT 'LOW'  OF STRUCTURE <fs_wa> TO <fs_wa_low>.
      ASSIGN COMPONENT 'HIGH' OF STRUCTURE <fs_wa> TO <fs_wa_high>.

      <fs_wa_low> = <fs_v_low>.
      <fs_wa_high> = <fs_v_high>.

      APPEND <fs_wa> TO <fs_it>.

      CLEAR: <fs_v_high>, <fs_wa>.

      <fs_v_low> = <fs_low>.
      PERFORM increase_1 USING <fs_low> CHANGING <fs_v_higher>.

    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.

  IF <fs_v_high> IS ASSIGNED AND
     <fs_v_high> IS NOT INITIAL.
    <fs_wa> = 'IBT'.
  ELSE .
    <fs_wa> = 'IEQ'.
  ENDIF.

  ASSIGN COMPONENT 'LOW'  OF STRUCTURE <fs_wa> TO <fs_wa_low>.
  ASSIGN COMPONENT 'HIGH' OF STRUCTURE <fs_wa> TO <fs_wa_high>.

  <fs_wa_low> = <fs_v_low>.
  <fs_wa_high> = <fs_v_high>.

  APPEND <fs_wa> TO <fs_it>.

  t_itab[] = <fs_it>.

ENDFUNCTION.


FORM increase_1 USING p_value CHANGING p_value_n.
  DATA: v_hx(1) TYPE x,
        v_len   TYPE i,
        v_off   TYPE i.

  p_value_n = p_value.

  FIELD-SYMBOLS <fs>.

*write:/ 'Initial:', p_value_n.
  v_len = strlen( p_value_n ).

  v_off = v_len - 1.

  DO v_len TIMES.

    IF p_value_n+v_off(1) BETWEEN 'A' AND 'Z'.
      IF p_value_n+v_off(1) LT 'Z'.
        ASSIGN p_value_n+v_off(1) TO <fs> CASTING TYPE x.
        ADD 1 TO <fs>.
        EXIT.
      ELSE.
        p_value_n+v_off(1) = 'A'.
      ENDIF.
    ELSEIF p_value_n+v_off(1) BETWEEN '0' AND '9'.
      IF p_value_n+v_off(1) LT '9'.
        p_value_n+v_off(1) = p_value_n+v_off(1) + 1.
        EXIT.
      ELSE.
        p_value_n+v_off(1) = '0'.
      ENDIF.
    ENDIF.
    v_off = v_off - 1.
  ENDDO.

*write:/ 'Final:', p_value_n.
ENDFORM.

Notes

The FM usage is pretty easy. Just call the FM in your development.

For standart programs, it has to be implemented with an enhancement or exit (if possible).

 

Conclusion

This FM is a plug and play design. But If you have any question, please ask. I will be checking the blog for Q&A. I am looking forward to share and learn.

entry.title: CDS Annotation: Dynamically hide a line_item
entry.content:
I have a simple CDS view with LINE_ITEM annotation. 

Dependent on a condition i want to hide the column via the hidden property. 

@ui.lineItem: [{ position: 1000, hidden: true }]  works fine. The column is not rendered. 

But none of the variants works all all:

 

@ui.lineItem: [{ position: 1000, hidden: #( 'X' )}]
@ui.lineItem: [{ position: 1000, hidden: #( true )}]

@ui.lineItem: [{ position: 1000, hidden: #( abap_true )}]

@ui.lineItem: [{ position: 1000, hidden: #( '1' )}]

Intention was to use something like:
@ui.lineItem: [{ position: 1000, hidden: #( case when MyTableField = '' then abap_true else abap_false end )}]

The question is, was is the correponding expression to "hidden: true"

entry.title: Re: S-user authorization to access Custom Code Analytics App
entry.content:
S-user IDs must be assigned the Service Reports and Feedback authorization in order to access the Custom Code Analytics application in SAP for Me.

For more information please refer to KBA: 3527942 - Granting Authorization to S User for Accessing Custom Code Analytics Page https://me.sap.com/notes/3527942

entry.title: Re: Still to be delivered value showing wrong for Service PO in ME23N
entry.content: If service PO is fully delivered (100% confirmation of planned service during service entry sheet), when you view progress in ME2L/ME2N the still to be delivered column will be zero. But if partially delivered then this column will show as per PO qty.

entry.title: Re: Request/task TH2K923438 cannot be deleted because it contains locked object

To unlock objects in particular request you should go to

  1. SE03 (Transport Organizer Tools) >> Requests/Tasks >> Unlock objects
  2. Select target request

Press Execute

P.S. You need S_CTS_ADMI authorization to get this menu item activated.

entry.title: Re: From Parallel Accounting to Universal Parallel Accounting and a focus on Parallel valuation
entry.content:
Hi @ABner, 

Thank you for your feedback on my blog. The parallel COGM function cannot be used, but this functionality is provided by UPA by having parallel valuation tied to ledgers - so we can calculate a standard cost for each ledger, and thereby have full E2E processes for each ledger - which replaces the classical COGM functionality. If your customer is already a S/4HANA customer and would like to use UPA, they can benefit from migrating to UPA, and if I am not mistaken, a migration scenario where a customer has COGM activated in their existing S/4HANA setup can move to UPA is already delivered. Ofcourse, their existing setup, scope etc needs to be studied. I don't know if it would be wise to give a "no-regrets setup" since every customer is different and their needs different.

entry.title: Extract KNVP Customer Master Partner Functions Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program
entry.content:
ABAP Program -  Z_KNVP_CUSTOMER       

  •  Program Type – ABAP 
  • ABAP Program Name – Z_KNVP_CUSTOMER 
  • Tcode - Z_KNVP_CUST
  • Entity Description - Customer Master Partner Functions  
  • Output File Location - “C:\tmp” 
  • Output File Name- KNVP.CSV 
  • Output File Type – CSV (Column with Semicolon separator (;) 
  • SAP Table Name – KNVP 
  • List of fields to be extracted -  
  • KUNNR 
  • PARVW 
  • KUNN2   

-----ABAP PROGRAM START FROM HERE---------------------------------

REPORT Z_KNVP_CUSTOMER.

TYPE-POOLS: VRM.

DATA: NAME TYPE VRM_ID,
LIST TYPE VRM_VALUES,
VALUE LIKE LINE OF LIST.


PARAMETERS: ENTITY(10) AS LISTBOX VISIBLE LENGTH 10.
PARAMETERS : OPATH TYPE STRING DEFAULT '\\SERVERNAME\tmp\KNVP.csv'.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.

NAME = 'ENTITY'.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK01'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK01'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK75'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK75'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK21'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK21'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK31'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK31'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK41'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK41'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK02'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK02'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK73'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK73'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK04'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK04'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK61'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK61'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.


CALL FUNCTION 'VRM_SET_VALUES' EXPORTING ID = NAME VALUES = LIST.

START-OF-SELECTION.

*&------WRITE: / 'LEGAL ENTITY NAME:', ENTITY.

*&------WRITE: / 'OUTPUT FILE PATH:', OPATH.

type-pools:TRUXS.

data: begin of itab occurs 0,
KUNNR like KNVP-KUNNR,
PARVW like KNVP-PARVW,
KUNN2 like KNVP-KUNN2,
BUKRS like KNB1-BUKRS,
end of itab.

data: itab1 type TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.
SELECT KNVP~KUNNR
KNVP~PARVW
KNVP~KUNN2
KNB1~BUKRS
FROM KNVP INNER JOIN KNB1 ON KNVP~KUNNR = KNB1~KUNNR
INNER JOIN KNA1 ON KNB1~KUNNR = KNA1~KUNNR
into table itab
WHERE KNB1~BUKRS = ENTITY.


CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT'
EXPORTING
I_FIELD_SEPERATOR = ','
TABLES
I_TAB_SAP_DATA = itab
CHANGING
I_TAB_CONVERTED_DATA = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
CONVERSION_FAILED = 1
OTHERS = 2
.
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.

CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
EXPORTING
filename = OPATH
TABLES
data_tab = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
OTHERS = 1.

entry.title: Re: Printing Multiple Invoices per Page in Crystal Reports
entry.content:
You can't do this manually laying out each field, it will only duplicate the same record.

Use Format with multiple columns feature by right clicking on Detail and selecting and check on the option:

Then drop each field in it's place for the first one, then copy all parts and paste into the next area...

And be sure to select a real printer, not one of MS's printers...

entry.title: Re: Link for subcontract purchase order and 541 Movement...
entry.content:
Hello @daria_garrido 

I don't think that a material movement providing a subcontractor with components is linked directly to subcontracting purchase orders. ME2O (I assume your ZME2O works the same) calculates net requirements of components to be provided and moves them to the subcontractor in bulk. Therefore several subcontracting POs can be supplied with a single material movement.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: SAP CAP & UI5 / Fiori Elements - State message handling recommendations / approach
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm looking for a solution to handle state messages in SAP CAP, combined with SAP Fiori Elements.

According to the UI5 documentation, state messages should be part of the response payload. https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/fbe1cb5613cf4a40a841750bf813238e.html#loiofbe1cb5613cf4a40a841750bf813238e/section_LMFSM

I'm in the situation where I have a business object modelled in CAP, which can contain multiple errors (background processing / jobs) where a user will finally need to make some manual changes to the object. Basically, clear case of state messages (messages don't change as long as the object doesn't change).

Currently it seems like CAP only supports transition messages out of the box with e.g. req.info, req.error...

I'd like to get to a situation where these messages are captured in the backend and provided as state messages to the frontend, so that when a user opens the object in change mode, the state messages are properly shown.

I couldn't find an example of this scenario and the CAP documentation doesn't seem to mention an approach  / recommendation for this scenario. 

Any pointers in the right direction are greatly appreciated!

Cheers,

Geert-Jan

entry.title: Supply Protection (SUP) in aATP - SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
Primarily, the objective of supply protection is to protect quantities of inventory and make the inventory prioritize for the incoming demand. Implementing supply protection allows the user to ensure that the essential supply gets assigned to key customer groups or requirements which receive from the chosen key characteristic.

Ex: An organization has 3 distribution channels which distribute their merchandize via E-Com (Online), W/S, RTL (Retail). Assume that, the sales margin for the merchandize distribution via E-Com is way higher than the other distribution channels. For an example let’s say, during the Christmas season, the business may require prioritizing the E-Com merchandise distribution, while the typical merchandize distribution via other distribution channels also should run smoothly. It is know that, order creations in sales order management process happens in sequential manure, where the prioritization characteristics are not considered at all during the process. Supply Protection allows you to protect your inventory for the prioritization characteristic which discussed above without any hassle. In other words, suppose you protect some quantities for the E-Com distribution channel due to the margin fact, quantities which you are going to protect will not be available for other distribution channels during product availability check execution upon sales order creation. Let’s dive into details along with SAP terminologies.

There are certain terminologies which will be essential to understand the core concept of supply protection where they are used in the system during the configuration as well as supply protection setup for fashion/ retail articles.

  • Supply Protection SUP - A method for safeguarding certain quantities of a material at a specific plant from other demands. Protection groups are established based on specific criteria and can be assigned priorities.
  • Supply protection object – This component is essential to trigger the supply protection during the product availability check execution. A supply protection object is designated for a single material-plant combination. Multiple supply protection objects can be managed for the same material-plant combination.
  • Core (horizontal) protection - A version of supply protection that presumes customers wish to safeguard entire groups from one another. Ex: The objective might be to safeguard specific markets like Germany or the UK. When a sales document is generated for the German market, the reserved quantity intended for the UK is unavailable for Germany or any other group.
  • Prioritized (vertical) protection - A type of supply protection used for planning quantities based on multiple characteristics and associated with different priorities. Ex: The objective might be to safeguard specific distribution channels like E-com, W/S or Retail. When a sales document is generated for the E-com distribution channel, the reserved quantity intended for the E-com is unavailable for W/S or any other channel.
  • Protected quantity - The quantity of material designated for a specific group must adhere to the demands of groups with equal or lower priority. Supply protection consistently utilizes the base unit of measure established in the material master record. This unit serves to define both protected and consumed quantities and is integral to the Available to Promise (ATP) check.
  • Consumed quantity - The quantity of confirmed sales documents or stock transport orders that diminishes the remaining protection (refer to “remaining protection” for further details).
  • Remaining protection - The sum of the unconsumed and unexpired protected quantities across individual time buckets. For instance, consider three time buckets, each with a protection of 200 pieces, and a consumption of 500 pieces occurring in time bucket 2. In this scenario, the remaining protected quantity totals 400 pieces (400 pieces from time bucket 1 and 400 pieces from time bucket 3), while the planned protected quantity remains at 600 pieces, and the consumed quantity is 500 pieces. Remaining protection is calculated at the level of time buckets, protection groups, and the supply protection object. A similar calculation is conducted for both consumption and protection.
  • Planning period/ Horizon - The time period during which protection data is maintained. This period is determined by the planning timeframe and the specified time intervals (such as weekly, monthly, etc.), which dictate the generation of time buckets.

Note: you might get a think that, why there are 2 protection objects as horizontal and vertical even though the protection objective can be achieved through 1 protection vertical (horizontal or vertical). That is because; the protection requirement could rise like below.

Ex: The objective might be to safeguard distribution for specific distribution channels like E-com, W/S or Retail for a specific market like Germany or UK. When a sales document is generated for the E-com distribution channel where the sold to party’s region in the sales document is Germany, the reserved quantity intended for the Germany- E-com is unavailable for Germany-W/S or any other horizontal + vertical protection combination. Let me explain it through the below matrix.

As you see in the above matrix, protection for W/S demand that comes from US is 1,500, which the W/S demand from UK should respect. And also protection for W/S demand that comes from UK is 3,000, which the W/S demand from US should respect.

Abbreviation explanation:

  • PRT for W/S - Protection for Wholesale distribution
  • PRT for RTL - Protection for Retail distribution
  • PRT for E-com - Protection for E-com distribution
  • Unre. St - Unrestricted/ unprotected stock
  • R. Qty - Required Quantity

As per the above illustration, 9000 & 4000 Pcs have been protected for E-Com and W/S channels accordingly. Even though the requirement is 3500 Pcs, it only utilizes the protected quantity for the RTL channel. Also, for the rest, it has utilized the unprotected/ unrestricted stock. 

Note: Once the planning horizon has expired, the protection object will no longer be active. Instead, all protections (vertical/ horizontal) of the relevant protection object will become irrelevant. 

Below is the primary application which you'll have to use in order to create the protection object.

App Name: Manage Supply Protection/ App Id: F4569

There is no specific app which allows you to mass maintain the supply protection objects. Refer to: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/15f9e51998a945cc82545cb6b4dbe5c2/ced8d281241040358bc54ba57baae01d.html

Prerequisite:

  • Supply Protection Activation in the availability check. (The availability check that you have maintained particularly in the article (For the relevant DC).)
  • Protection Object maintenance. (Check for the above information) 

The following document types are supported by the active supply protection:

  • Sales documents
  • Stock transport orders
  • Contracts
  • Scheduling agreements
  • Deliveries free of charge

When setting up the vertical and horizontal protections, if you are unable to find the characteristic that you need to protect your supply under, Manage Characteristics Catalog app allows you to customize your characteristics selection during the protection object creation.

Also, it is very important to understand the significant difference in between Supply Protection and Product Allocation. Please reach out to me for a detailed explanation on this regard.

 
Useful Links: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/f132c385e0234fe68ae9ff35b2da178c/c4b704762cbd4611a3ee2dc00c7a7277.html

entry.title: SAP MM Questionnaire
entry.content:
When it comes to implementing SAP MM (Material Management), a detailed and well-thought-out questionnaire is essential. Whether you’re embarking on a new SAP implementation or rolling out SAP MM to multiple entities, crafting the right questions can provide deep insights into business needs, highlight potential gaps, and ensure smooth system integration. In this blog, we’ll explore the key components of a comprehensive SAP MM questionnaire and why it’s crucial for successful project execution.

1. Organization  
1.1. Purchasing Group Do purchasing groups represent individual buyers or groups of buyers?
Please provide a list of groups with responsibilities 
1.2. Purchasing Organization Which purchasing departments exist in your enterprise?
If there is more than one department which handles all purchasing, specify which department(s) negotiate pricing terms and conditions with your suppliers.  
How do the departments share the task of procuring the goods and services required by the organization? 
Which material types/external services do you procure? 
Where do you procure materials/services in your enterprise?  
Do you negotiate vendor pricing at a corporate or local level?  
1.3. Plants and storage locationsDefinition of plant types and business rules behind
Provide list of plants 
Clarify aspects of Sloc Division (30 Unique and 771 total) 
Provide list of storage locations with designation   
2. Master Data  
1.1. Materials How many different materials do you procure on a regular basis? 
Please provide material master data structure, fields used including classification, custom fields if any 
Please describe material master management process 
What is the reporting / analytics used for material master data 
1.2. VendorsPlease provide vendor master data structure, fields used including classification, custom fields if any
Please describe vendor master management process 
Is there any external integration for material/vendor master data (data received or sent to external systems/parties) 
What is the reporting / analytics used for vendor master data 
1.1. Conditions Which price components do you use in purchasing documents (e.g. gross price, surcharges, discounts, freight, duty, import)? 
Do prices depend on the quantity ordered (e.g. quantity discounts or price scales)? If so, specify price components, quantity, and other factors 
Do you manually change the price at header level for the entire purchasing document 
Do you allow changes to the gross price that is automatically determined by the System?  
Does the vendor's price include value-added tax?  
Is it possible to change pricing conditions after a PO has been created?  
1.2. Info recordsWhat is the process of maintaining purchasing info records? 
Are info records updated automatically with every purchase order?  
1.3. Source List Do you maintain a list of approved vendors? This means that if a material is subject to a source list requirement, it can only be procured from the vendors included on the source list. 
Are you using source list for: (1) all materials (2) all materials of a plant (3) certain materials only? Please explain 
Are there fixed vendor for some materials? 
Is it be possible to for purchase orders to be created automatically in the course of material requirements planning (MRP)?  
Should it be possible to for scheduling agreement schedule lines to be created automatically in the course of material requirements planning (MRP)?  
1.4. Quota Arrangement Do you maintain specific vendor quotas (for example 70 % from vendor A, 30% from vendor B) for your materials? If yes, how many quota arrangements are you currently maintaining? 
 1.5. Release Strategy
 with Classification 		Are any purchasing documents  subject to approval in SAP? If yes, describe the criteria that are needed to determine the appropriate approval policy for purchase requistions, RFQs, purchase orders, contracts, scheduling agreements. 
1.6. Vendor Evaluation How do you evaluate vendors in your legacy system? 
Which criteria do you use to evaluate your vendors?  
3. Sourcing and operational procurement (fuels, lubricants related, indirect) 
3.1. Purchase Requisition How are purchase requisitions created in the case of stock material, material for direct consumption, external services? 
Do you use a purchase requisition to trigger creation of for a contract or scheduling agreement (Outline Agreement Request)?  
Are purchase requisitions generated via material/article requirements planning  manually post-processed?  
On the basis of which criteria are purchase requisitions grouped together and assigned to a source of supply? 
What support (e.g. price simulation) does the buyer need in order to assign the purchase requisition?  
Are purchase requisitions converted into requests for quotations?  
Should requisitions be subject to approval by someone (or possibly several people) before the requisition can be processed into an RFQ and/or purchase order?     
How is the person responsible for releasing the purchase requisition to be notified?  
3.2. Purchasing processHow many different materials do you procure on a regular basis? 
How are purchase orders created in your system?  
What are the types of purchase orders in use? What is the specific process for each type 
Describe how the source of supply is determined for manually created purchase requisitions 
Do you create RFQs in the system? How are your RFQs and rejection letters to your vendors to be transmitted?  
Do you use contracts / scheduling agreements? What is the rule to create each type of document 
Are scheduling agreement delivery schedules/SA releases created in SAP manually, with reference to requisitions and/or automatically?  
Which kinds of purchasing document do you send to vendors? I.e. 1) The complete purchasing  document 2) Information re changes 3) Reminders (prior to due date 4) Urging letters/expediters (after due date) 5) Scheduling agreement delivery schedules.  
Are purchase documents approved by someone before being issued to vendors? Describe the approval procedure 
How is the person responsible for approval notified? - Approver checks regularly, by phone, by e-mail, by workflow, other 
How do you transmit purchase orders to your vendors? Please provide examples of printouts, idocs, etc. 
Create a list of which SAP messages are sent to which business partners, which IDoc type is used and which EDI standards the messages are to be converted into.  
Once you have issued a purchasing document to a vendor, do you track "confirmations" your vendor may return to you regarding the order?  
Which type of vendor confirmation do you need and at which time intervals? Which events should trigger a confirmation?  
Do you process shipping notifications with the SAP System? How do your vendors transmit shipping notifications? What information does the shipping notification contain?  
Do you plan and enter freight costs in the PO? If yes, describe the basis of the costs. Also indicate if any types of costs can be determined automatically
 (for example, freight costs per piece, per unit of weight, as a percentage of the value). 		 
What types of indirect (not related to sales and/or production) materials and services do you procure? 
Do you order stock material directly for a cost center or another consumption category?  
Specify the consumption categories for which you procure external services and material directly: Asset, cost center, production order, project, sales order, other (please specify).  
Can the materials you purchase be subject to different tax types? (For example,
based upon the material purchased, based upon the plant for which the material is
purchased, etc)?                                                                            		 
Is it to be possible for purchase orders to be generated automatically following a goods receipt? Specify the criteria for this 
What are the types of reports being used and analytics done on purchasing data? 
Are there any integrations with external systems / external parties of purchasing data and documents? 
How do the procurement flows differ between raw materials (base oil, additives) and finished lubricants? 
Are there any long-term agreements or framework contracts in place for base oil or additives procurement? 
How are pricing conditions managed for these categories, especially considering fluctuations in base oil prices? 
How do you handle supplier lead times and procurement planning for critical inputs like base oil and additives? 
Are there any pain points in the current release or approval process that slow down procurement? 
3.3 Goods Receipt Processing Describe the process for receiving goods with reference to a purchase order
Name the storage locations types (incl. stock types Unrestricted-Blocked- Quality) to which vendors deliver the goods. 
How do you inform the Purchasing Department that goods have been received? 
Do you receive quantities less than the ordered quantity? If yes, is the purchase order considered complete then or do you receive the missing quantities later? 
Do you physically store the goods you have received into "stock in quality inspection" at a different location than those posted to normal stock? 
Do the materials you receive have to be stored for a certain time before they can be used or do they have an expiration date that you want to keep in the system? 
Do you refuse to accept deliveries if the vendor has not complied with the shipping instructions? 
Which documents are generated with the goods receipt? 
If you are using batch management, how is the batch number determined at the time of goods receipt? 
Do you classify the batches at the time of goods receipt? Please specify the criteria 
Do you wish to print out the material document as evidence of a goods movement? Which information should be included in the printout? 
3.4. Service Entry SheetHow are services performed by the service provider processed in the system?
3.5. Return DeliveryDo you create action plans/tasks for the Returns?
Which documentation should accompany the goods that are returned to the vendor 
3.6. Invoice VerificationHow are invoices created in the system (manual, automatic processing)
According to which rules do you effect automatic settlement with regard to goods receipts? 
Does a goods receipt with reference to a PO automatically generate an invoice for the delivery? 
Create a list of which SAP messages are sent to which business partners, which IDoc type is used and which EDI standards the messages are to be converted into. 
Are down payments also  cleared? 
Which other messages do you want to send to your vendors or to people within your organization with respect to the invoice receipt? 
Is a goods receipt always necessary for invoice verification purposes? If so, do you allow reversal of the goods issue after the invoice has been posted? has been entered 
Which invoice blocking reasons are used? 
4. Stock transfers 
4.1. Stock Transport Order (STO)What is the purpose of using STO in your organization?(e.g., inter-company transfers, plant-to-plant transfers, cross-company code stock movements)
What types of materials/products are typically transferred using STO? 
Which locations or plants are involved in stock transfers? 
What are the expected lead times for stock transfers? 
How is transportation handled for stock movements? (e.g., own fleet, third-party logistics) 
Is there a requirement for purchase orders (PO) during STO transactions? 
What is the process for issuing goods for stock transfer? (e.g., automatic goods issue based on delivery note, manual goods issue) 
Is there a requirement for quality checks or inspections at the receiving plant? 
How is stock valued in your organization during transfers? 
Are there any specific workflow approvals needed for STO creation or processing? 
5. Inventory Management 
5.1 Reservation Processing Do you maintain specific material reservations (for sales, for production, other issues) for your stock materials?
How far in advance are materials to be allowed to be reserved 
How long after the reservation date are open reservations canceled? 
5.2. Inventory Valuation and TrackingWhat method of inventory valuation do you use? (e.g., standard price, moving average price)
Do you use split valuation? For what purpose 
How do you currently track inventory in real time? 
Is there a requirement to differentiate between unrestricted stock and blocked stock? 
How do you manage different units of measure? 
What reporting you use for inventory tracking / analytics 
5.3. Stock Availability and PlanningHow do you manage stock availability across multiple locations?
Do you need alerts or notifications for stock shortages? 
What are the key metrics you track in inventory management? (e.g., safety stock, reorder points) 
5.4. Goods Movements and TransfersWhat types of movements are used? Any special procedures for GRs, GIs, transfers; custom movement types?
How are order confirmations from suppliers managed? 
How do you handle partial deliveries and over-deliveries? 
What processes are involved in goods receipts and goods issues for STOs? 
Are there any quality checks or sampling procedures before goods receipts are finalized for lubricants, base oil, or additives? 
Are there specific movement types required for stock transfers? (e.g., 301 for plant-to-plant transfer, 641 for stock transport order with delivery) 
5.5. Batch and Serial Number ManagementDo you require batch management or serialization for inventory items?
How do you assign and manage batch numbers incl. for stock transfers? 
Do batches expire, and how is shelf life managed? 
5.6. Physical InventoryHow often do you perform physical inventory checks?
Which inventory items or locations are prioritized for physical stocktaking 
How do you handle discrepancies found during physical inventory? (e.g., automatic adjustment, investigation process) 
What approval workflows are required for adjusting inventory balances? 
Do you use automatic postings for inventory adjustments? 
Which method do you use for physical inventory? (e.g., continuous inventory, periodic inventory) 
Do you have a specific process for high-value items or critical stock? 
WRICEFIdentify WRICEF elements (Workflows, Reports, Interfaces, Conversions, Enhancements, and Forms) and their Counts.

entry.title: Automating SAP Post-Refresh Steps with SAP GUI Scripting (Part 2)
entry.content:
In Part 1, I introduced the basics of automating SAP post-refresh tasks using SAP GUI scripting. In Part 2, we will dive into more advanced automation tasks, focusing on key transactions (T-codes) that simplify common post-refresh activities. Each T-code is paired with a scripting example to show how these steps can be automated efficiently. 

T-Code: SM35 - Batch Input Monitoring (Job Log Deletion)

How It Helps: Post-refresh, outdated job logs need to be removed to prevent conflicts or unnecessary storage usage. Manually deleting job logs can be tedious, especially in larger systems. Automating this step ensures that the system starts clean after the refresh.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nsm35"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/btn%_UABLG_%_APP_%-VALU_PUSH").press
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[16]").press  ' Deletes job logs

T-Code: SU01 - User Management (Password Update)

How It Helps: User passwords often need to be reset after a system refresh to maintain security and access control. Automating this process is critical when managing multiple user accounts.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nsu01"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtSUID_ST_BNAME-LOW").text = "USERNAME"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/btn%_BUTTON").press  ' Updates password

T-Code: BDLS - Conversion of Logical System Names

How It Helps: After a system copy or refresh, logical system names need to be updated to reflect the new environment. Automating the BDLS conversion reduces the risk of errors and ensures that all necessary updates are completed efficiently.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nbdls"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/txtBDLS_FROM").text = "OLD_LOGSYS"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/txtBDLS_TO").text = "NEW_LOGSYS"
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[8]").press  ' Start BDLS Conversion

T-Code: DBCO - Database Connections Maintenance

How It Helps: After a refresh, database connections need to be re-established. Automating this process ensures that connections are properly configured, reducing the risk of connection issues post-refresh.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/ndbco"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/btn%_CONNECTION_%_APP_%-VALU_PUSH").press
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/txtDBCOTXT-CONN").text = "CONNECTION_NAME"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/txtDBCOTXT-USER").text = "USERNAME"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/txtDBCOTXT-PASS").text = "PASSWORD"
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[11]").press  ' Save DB Connection

T-Code: SMLG - Logon Group Configuration

How It Helps: Configuring logon groups helps distribute the user workload across multiple servers. Automating this process ensures that the correct logon groups are set up after the refresh, optimizing system performance.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nsmlg"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtGROUP_NAME").text = "LOGON_GROUP"
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/ctxtSERVER_NAME").text = "SERVER_NAME"
session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[1]/btn[11]").press  ' Save Logon Group

T-Code: ST03 - Workload Analysis

How It Helps: Running workload analysis helps identify performance bottlenecks in the system after a refresh. Automating the ST03 transaction helps streamline the analysis, ensuring that performance data is available immediately.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nst03"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/btn%_ANALYZE_%").press  ' Start workload analysis

T-Code: TEMSE - TemSe Object Management

How It Helps: Managing TemSe objects, which store spool data, is crucial to avoid performance issues. Automating the cleanup or management of TemSe objects ensures smooth spool processing post-refresh.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/ntemse"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/btn%_MANAGE_TEMSE_%").press  ' Manage TemSe objects

T-Code: Import PSEs

How It Helps: After a system refresh, importing PSEs (Personal Security Environment) for secure communication may be necessary. Automating this ensures that security settings are applied correctly and without manual errors.

Script Example:

session.findById("wnd[0]/tbar[0]/okcd").text = "/nimport_pse"
session.findById("wnd[0]").sendVKey 0
session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/btn%_IMPORT_%").press  ' Import PSEs

 

Conclusion

By automating key post-refresh tasks in SAP using GUI scripting, you can significantly reduce manual efforts, minimize errors, and accelerate the system stabilization process. This guide has provided detailed examples of automating crucial T-codes like SMLG, DBCO, BDLS, and more, to streamline post-refresh activities. These scripts not only help ensure consistency across the system but also enable administrators to focus on more strategic tasks by automating repetitive and time-consuming actions.

entry.title: Upgrading from SAP Build community tier, i need help!
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I use SAP Build apps for quite some time now, almost 3 years. I want to escalate business and hire some people to help me develop my applications, but the Community version only allow one user per account.

I read the pricing page about the enterprise plan but I don't know how those features plans compare to the features in the community version, do I necessary need an Entitlement Package? Even with the description I didn't understand its use, I want a Manager account with 2~10 users to use the SAP Build Apps solution and features like I have Community in my account, how do I calculate and get and estimated price? The information available was a little confused because the estimated pricing was ranging between EUR 1,000.00 to EUR 1,300.00, its very expansive step-up for someone who use the community version.

Any help and information on how to start will help.

Thanks everyone!

entry.title: Incorporating Custom Events from Lobby into SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Introduction

What is SAP Build? 

  • SAP Build is a cloud-based tool for designing and prototyping applications with ease
  • User-friendly interface and drag-and-drop functionality make interactive prototypes without extensive coding
  • Supports real-time collaboration, seamless integration with other SAP solutions, and pre-built templates

In this blog, our goal is to explore the process of uploading event specifications to SAP Build Lobby and consuming those events in SBPA.

SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) enables business users to automate workflow processes and tasks without writing code.

Check here for What's New and Learn more from the roadmap.  

This August 2024, SAP Build Process Automation has a released a new way of discovering Business Events from the Lobby.

Check out this blog...

Discovering Business Events from Lobby for SAP Build Process Automation: Manage Credit Memo Request 


Creating Event Projects from the Lobby: By Uploading Event Specification...

  • Navigate to the Connector Section in the Lobby:

First, you can access the Lobby section of SAP Build and navigate to the Connector area. This is where you will be able to manage and consume events within your application.

  • Events Have a Dedicated Place and Subpage:

Once in the Connector section, you will find that events have their dedicated place and subpage. This makes it easier for you to manage and work with different types of events for your application.

  • In this guide, we will focus on the Upload specification source when adding an event.
    As a user, you will see the create button on the Events subpage.

  • Events can be easily uploaded into the lobby of our platform.
    Presently, we support only JSON format for event specifications, with plans to expand to additional formats in the future.

For demonstration purposes, I have downloaded an example from BAH to illustrate this process. 

  1. Go to the Business Accelerator Hub page. 
  2. Select any of the products --> for this example -  SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  3. Select events on the tab below the Title and Short description.
    1. Select any event object that you wish --> for this example - Business Situation Events



  4. Download the file to your system. 

Any events in JSON format can be added, considering that the event mesh supports that specific event.

  • Event spec file uploaded will consists of Events and the Payload of Events:

    Each uploaded event object contains the event itself and its associated payload. This information is crucial for understanding the type and scope of each event that you are consuming.






  • Add the Event Object to the Build Lobby:

    Once you upload the desired event, you can easily view it in the Build Lobby.
    This will make the event accessible within your application.

 

  • Events can be Used Across Build Products:

    Currently, the Build Process Automation is utilizing uploaded events, with plans for Build Apps to also make use of uploaded events in the near future. This means that once you upload an event in the Lobby, it can potentially be utilized across different SAP Build products.

  • Discovering Events via Library in the Process Builder:

    After uploading an event in the Lobby, you can discover and utilize it through the library in the Process Builder. This ensures that the event is easily accessible for building and automating processes within your application.


 

Other use cases…

There are some additional steps to setup Event Mesh 
Event Mesh and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Setup for SAP Build Process Automation

entry.content:

entry.title: Payroll Canada Year End 2024
entry.content:
Payroll Canada Year End 2024

Greetings from SAP HCM Canada Support team!

On this blog we will share news, important notes and changes related to Canada Year End 2024. If you have questions about 2024 Canada Year End, feel free to ask in the comment section of this blog!

 

Legal Changes for HCM Canada

To review the upcoming legal changes for Canada you can access the Regulatory Change Manager. 

Please check the KBA 3421995 - Using the regulatory change manager tool to find if a solution is planned for a legal change for more information.

 

ASUG Webcast

SAP HCM Canada Payroll and Tax Reporting Year-End Update 2024:

Date: October 30th, 2024

Time: 12:30 PM CDT

 

Speakers:

  • Viswanath Ramamurthy, Product Manager, HCM US & Canada, SAP Labs
  • Boris Cruz, Senior Developer, SAP

More information and registration in the following page:

SAP HCM Canada Payroll and Tax Reporting Year-End Update 2024

 

Main SAP Note and minimum mandatory HRSP for Year End 2024

3513929 - Main SAP Note for Canadian year-end 2024

The minimum mandatory HRSP for Canadian Year End in fiscal year 2024 is specified in the above Note, according to the software release.

The system must be updated to the minimum required HRSP to work correctly. If you face an issue that is caused by not having the minimum HRSP, we will suggest the HRSP upgrade.

You can find Support Packages and CLCs schedules in the following page:

SAP ERP Human Capital Management Support Package Schedules

Please constantly check the main note as it will be updated throughout the year-end to reflect the most up-to-date information on Year End 2024 processing, including new correction notes.

You can set a notification to know when a note/KBA is updated or released. You can check the KBA 2478289 - How to set up notifications for new or updated SAP Notes and/or KBAs with Expert Search filters - SAP for Me for more information

 

Reporting a tax calculation issue

In case you identify issues with tax calculation, please report a case and provide your analysis as per the approach below:

3314325 - How to report tax calculation issues in SAP HCM Canada?

We do not accept comparison with PDOC, as this calculator cannot simulate all aspects of payroll of SAP HCM.

2658386 - Differences in tax calculation between SAP and CRA PDOC

 

Relevant KBAs

1974838 - T4 not displaying lower half of form in run viewer

3433906 - Why the year end report does not print the current form version?

3117398 - Change in employee's name not generating T4/T4A/T4A-NR amendment

 

 

Frequently Asked Questions

1. When is the PIER report going to be updated to be compliant with CPP2 (CPP second contribution)?

The PIER report was updated by the note 3512979 - YE24: PIER Report for second additional CPP/QPP contribution to be compliant with CPP2.

 

Questions and feedback

Your feedback and constructive criticism are appreciated.

As already mentioned, feel free to comment on this post if you have questions about 2024 Canada Year End.

 

Kind regards, 

Claudia, Carlos and Jonas.

entry.title: Perform the Iterative Simulation in SAC using Data Actions with blazing fast performance (Goal-Seek)
entry.content:
Need for Enhanced Simulation Capabilities in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC)

In today's fast-paced and highly competitive business landscape, the ability to make informed, data-driven decisions is crucial for organizations aiming to differentiate themselves. One key aspect of effective decision-making is the capacity to evaluate the potential impacts of various choices before they are implemented. To this end, many managers are increasingly seeking tools that facilitate in-depth analysis of different outcomes based on varying assumptions.

Currently, while SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) provides robust analytics capabilities, it falls short in offering comprehensive iterative simulation functionalities. Features such as goal-seeking and what-if analysis are essential for businesses to assess the implications of their decisions dynamically. Goal-seeking allows users to determine the necessary inputs to achieve a desired outcome, while what-if analysis empowers them to explore various scenarios and their potential impacts on business performance

The absence of these simulation capabilities in SAC limits the ability of organizations to conduct thorough scenario planning and optimize their strategies effectively. As companies face complex challenges—ranging from market volatility to changing consumer preferences—the need for a solution that integrates simulation functionalities into SAC has become more pressing than ever.

By implementing enhanced simulation capabilities, SAC can enable managers to:

  1. Explore Multiple Scenarios: Evaluate how different variables impact outcomes, fostering a deeper understanding of potential risks and opportunities.
  2. Make Informed Decisions: Use data-driven insights to guide strategic choices, leading to more effective and optimized outcomes.
  3. Adapt to Changing Conditions: Quickly assess the ramifications of external changes, allowing businesses to pivot and respond proactively.

In conclusion, enhancing SAP Analytics Cloud with robust simulation functionalities such as goal-seek and what-if analysis is essential for empowering organizations to navigate today’s complex business environment. By equipping managers with the tools to simulate, analyse, and adapt their decisions, SAC can truly become an invaluable asset in optimizing business performance.

Although, we can achieve real-time simulations using Member Formulas, Story calculations and VDTs, we also need to understand, in xP&A world, we would need to have this capability to have the result of these simulations saved in our data model. So, when we use Data Actions for these functionalities, like simulations, Goal-seek or What if analysis, it would be very lengthy and time-consuming processes.

Refer:

Simulation on SAC with What-If analysis :  https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-if-analysis-with-sap-analytics-cloud-simulate-your-future-business/ba-p/13475295

Simulation on SAC Live Models : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-if-simulation-in-a-sac-story-based-on-a-sac-live-model-e-g-sap-bw/ba-p/13525485

Simulation with VDT : https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/leveraging-sap-analytics-cloud-functionality-for-enterprise-planning/simulating-with-value-driver-trees_fc3552eb-ceb0-4f96-93e4-7c690bcaed2d

 

In any CAPEX decision, first thing they would like to see, is how is the current situation and how much they can benefit, if they improve on certain things. So, in nutshell, there will be certain conditions or constraints (inputs) and there will be some outcomes based on these inputs.

And businesses has to decide, what input they can afford, to get the desired outcome or vice versa.

Many businesses, use the Excel’s native function of Goal-Seek, which changes the Conditions (input) until the goal is achieved. and they start comparing the SAC solution with Excel and expect the simulation results in few seconds and also saved for use in other processes.

Goal-Seek is nothing but, If you know the result that you want from a formula but are not sure what input value the formula needs to get that result, use the Goal Seek feature.

This is known as iterative simulation. Where you simulate the results again and again until you reach to your goal by changing the input gradually in every iteration of simulation.

So Let us start how can we achieve the Iterative Simulation in SAC where we store the results of Every Iteration, and then find the goal. This Blog will focus mostly on Design, which can be implemented for any use case or scenario, where goal-seek is required.

In below example, for a power plant,

Goal is = Set NPV (Net Present Value) to 0

By Changing = Capacity Charge (Generation Cost - Input) X

It involves around 50+ Business logic to calculate the NPV from the Capacity Charge (generation cost).

Here Capacity Charge is critical business parameter, which decides the viability of project before we invest in project.

So below diagram explains how typically any Goal-Seek would work.

 

So now we understand that we can split the entire process in three areas,

  1. before goalseek (which are necessary to goalseek but may not be part of direct calculations of Goalseek KPIs.)
  2. GoalSeek KPIs itself
  3. After Goalseek

This way we can have better control over the Goalseek Process.

It is always necessary to ensure that we build data actions as per best practices, like scoping in every step, not leaving any dimension undefine, avoiding loops, choosing valid hierarchy etc so that we get smaller runtime and better performances.

Now let us assume that one of your goalseek Data Action is taking around the 80 seconds, so for one hundred iterations it would take around 8000 seconds. Which is almost a two hour and half.

This longer wait time makes business leaders disappointed and not user-friendly tool. However, we know that SAC analytics cloud runs on the SAP HANA database, here comes the power of SAP HANA Columnar processing capabilities. I have designed the solution which does the parallel simulation of these iterations and thus leveraging the SAP HANA processing with Innovative design of parallel processing of these iterations.

So, what we need to do is, we need to enable our system to perform the parallel processing of the all the iterations of Goalseek. Because the logic of Goalseek for all the iterations remain same.

Below Diagram explains how to parallelize the goalseek.

  1. First generate the required KPIs for Goalseek Calculations (which are not dependent of the user input Capacity Charge.)
  2. Perform Goalseek Calculations parallelly for multiple iterations (configurable)
  3. Find the correct Goal from above step 2 (Goalseek)
  4. Perform the next dependent steps (Optional)

 

 

 

Let us see steps. Technically now:

  1. Add the Iteration Dimension in your existing Model. (Where you want to parallelize the simulation results)

Since Goal-Seek Needs to perform N no. of iterations, we need to identify the iteration with the different identifier.

Example of Iteration Dimension:

 

In above example we have added the Iteration Identifier in Member IDs. But most importantly, we need to add the criteria of every iteration. Which is you want to change the certain input by x amount. In our case we have added the incremental change by 0.0000001 for every iteration member. See above table. You may add multiple properties similarly with different criterial of incremental change and you can achieve the different simulation possible with same data.

 

  1. Prepare the DATA

Default Value for all the data records in the Model after adding the ITERATION dimension will be #. Perform the Before Goalseek Preparatory steps (if any) and store all the results.

  1. Run Parallel Goalseek

Now we need to perform the Goalseek in parallel process.

So, we need to have the Pre-Requisite records generated at above step B, need to be replicated for no. of Iterations.

So, we need to create a Copy Steps from # iteration to multiple Iterations as shown in below screen.

 

  1. Execute the same Goalseek KPI Data Action step (which earlier was used to run for 80 seconds for single iteration) with required no. of iterations.

Now we need to mention the Iteration filter in

MEMBERSET [d/ZD_ITERATION] = BASEMEMBER([d/ZD_ITERATION], %Define_Goalseek_Iterations%)

 

 

 

Remember that we don’t want to run the GoalSeek KPIs for # Iteration. So, we are not including that in this step.

Here we have created a parameter for user to select the no. of iterations. User can select either 50 or 100 …. Iterations. And based on the member selected, DATA action will run for those many Iterations (Set of Data) parallelly. And will calculate the Goalseek KPI, which is NPV in our case.

One User Enters the Reference Capacity Charge (input) and we need to perform the Simulation (Goalseek KPIs) for multiple iterations, with different capacity charge, based on the user inputted initial value.

There fore When First time this user inputted value is used inside our data action logic, we need to change it for every iteration, based on its respective iterative change value we maintained in master data.

In our case we are changing it by 0.0000001, 0.0000002, 0.0000003….

Refer Iteration Dimension Screenshot and below Data Action screenshot.

 

And thus, we have run the Goalseek Simulation for all the possible combinations (no. or iterations in our case is 100) and the final value for all those 100 sets is calculated and stored against the respective iteration number.

  1. Identify the Correct NPV=0 (Goal)

Now we need to identify the correct Iteration Set where the required goal is achieved and same we need to copy to # Iteration so that we have the correct Goal in its respective capacity charge and Goalseek KPIs available for all other reporting and further calculations.

So, we need to Loop on each iteration to find the KPI NPV is value. Wherever we find the NPV=0, we will break the loop and capture the respective loop no. and store in Last Iteration Measure and Store the Correct Capacity Charge, which is User input capacity Charge less the respective Iterations’ reduction value from master data.

And lastly, we copy all results of that iteration set to # iteration, so that all further KPIs and reporting can be done.

Now the Data on non # Iteration can be deleted if business no longer wants it.

 

So Thus we can see that Even with Data Action Running for 100 Iterations, it will not take much time.

in our case, single iteration took around 70-80 sec, but with this parallel approach it takes only 2.5 min for 100 iterations, which means only 1.5 sec per iteration. in our case single iteration generates around 1.2 k records in 80 seconds, so 100 iteration it generated 122k records in just  2min. In total it improved performance by 98%

 

  1.

entry.title: A new approach to SAP authorization roles – explained using LEGO® as an example
entry.content:
Creating and managing authorization roles in SAP S/4HANA is a complex and time-consuming process. Month-long projects that require a great deal of resource commitment are the norm. However, there is another way that significantly simplifies and accelerates this process.

A scientifically based approach to role creation

Our method for creating SAP authorization roles is based on a scientifically sound modularity that allows role building blocks to be put together flexibly and precisely. Just as LEGO® building blocks can be sorted and grouped, our approach allows authorizations to be built so that they can be aligned with the needs of the company in a consistently compliant manner, independently of the organization and company. At the same time, they must be scalable quickly and easily in order to map rollouts and changes without incurring high costs.

The technical background

The approach we developed over 15 years ago is based on the principles of system theory and modular software architecture. We chose a mathematical approach and described it in the form of an equivalence relation. We applied these findings to the SAP authorization logic from R/3 to today's S/4HANA Public Cloud, creating a way to segregate and provide roles in a highly efficient and resource-saving manner.

What makes this method special?

  • Modularity: Roles are broken down into building blocks (5-tuples) based on characteristics relevant to segregation, such as access mode, line of business, price category, business process area, and criticality, while taking into account the constraints that the number of roles must be minimal and element pairs that are on the prohibition list (SoD) must not occur in the same group. These subsets can be combined and adapted as desired, as long as they do not violate compliance, which MUST be defined.
  • Efficiency: Modularity not only reduces implementation effort by more than 80%, but also future maintenance.
  • Uniqueness: This approach is globally unique and does not use any additional tools, but simply a method based on the SAP standard in each system.

The formula as a narrative explained using LEGO® as an example

We have a huge pile of Lego bricks. They represent the functions in SAP S/4HANA ERP

We have the task of sorting all these bricks into groups according to the following rules:

  1. In each group, all bricks with the same number of stud rows (access type), the same colors (LoB), shapes (square, rectangular and round (license), sizes (business process area - SW components/package affiliation)
  2. Stones with a structured surface correspond to our criticality C5 “very critical” in separate groups
  3. Stone pairs must not be in the same group according to the definition of set V of forbidden pairs
  • Form as few groups as possible to save space and keep an overview (condition |R| is minimal)
  • Groups must be split if we find stone pairs that must not be together according to the list of prohibitions
  • Make sure that the stones with a structured surface remain separate (very high criticality)
  • Try to keep as many similar stones together as possible, as long as they are not on the forbidden list.

Result 🎯

A series of boxes (subsets) only with stones with identical properties, except for the surface finish, where the very rough or structured surface stones are separated.

Each stone is in exactly one box, and you have used as few boxes as possible.

Advantages for SAP CoE

For SAP managers, this approach means a significant reduction in project effort and thus significant cost reduction. Authorization roles are clearly structured and can be quickly adapted to changing requirements, which simplifies long-term maintenance. And they are compliant!

Discussion and exchange

Perhaps there are other approaches that redefine the limits of what is possible and make the seemingly impossible possible. I would be interested to hear about them. I welcome any exchange of ideas. I learn by listening and sharing knowledge.

If you like, you can watch the video, which explains the method on a slightly less technical level: Is LEGO® revolutionizing the SAP S/4HANA authorization chaos?

Note: translated with DeepL
